steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence: "Sea otters use stationary anvil stones to break open mollusk shells." It follows the claim that "other animals also find tools helpful," so its job is structural — what role does this sentence play in the paragraph?

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It argues that sea otters use tools more often than other animals do.’: The sentence makes no comparison of frequency between sea otters and other animals. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It provides one example of tool use in a nonhuman animal.’: The sentence names a specific animal (sea otters) and a specific tool use (anvil stones for mollusks) — a concrete example supporting the preceding claim. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘It emphasizes that sea otters are smarter than birds.’: The sentence says nothing about birds or relative intelligence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It explains that humans use tools for many different tasks.’: The sentence is about sea otters, not humans. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
